stages.purpose: Search both name-and-location pairs in the same assistant action; all inputs are known and neither search depends on the other.
Only after stage 1 returns one match for a branch, use that returned ID in a later assistant action; when both branches resolve, the two gets are independent and belong together.

state_assertions: No record, notification log, or mutation log changes.
tel-958 and tel-858 remain untouched distractors.
Each get ID equals the sole match returned by its own search; no ID is inferred from the prompt or fixture.
response_constraints: Base the comparison only on successful get responses, not on abbreviated search matches.
For a branch with zero or multiple search matches, omit its get; still complete any independent branch with exactly one match and identify the unresolved branch.
Do not expose distractor records or propose or perform changes.
Keep the reply under 75 words, include both stable IDs, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”

prompt: The service desk is reconciling two subscription records for the account review. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the subscription named “Depot Radio Data Subscription” at “Fleet Operations” and the subscription named “Library Hotspot Subscription” at “Outreach Services”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Keep the reply under 75 words, include both stable IDs, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”
